forecasts.primary_concern: Wind-drifted snow and wet loose activity.
forecasts.discussion: Upper elevation bands are characterized by cold temperatures (6.1F) and moderate winds (12.4 mph), facilitating wind slab development on leeward North and East aspects. At middle elevations, significant solar radiation (723 W/m2) and liquid water content (0.25) on Southern aspects indicate surface snow softening. Lower elevations received the greatest 24-hour snowfall (3.7 inches), though stability remains moderate across all bands with a danger level of 2.
forecasts.travel_advice: Avoid steep Southern aspects during peak solar hours due to increasing liquid water content. Exercise caution on North and East-facing leeward slopes at upper elevations where wind slabs may be reactive. The highest quality and safest snow is located on sheltered Northern aspects at middle elevations where cold content remains stable.

forecasts.primary_concern: New snow accumulation and solar-driven instability.
forecasts.discussion: Upper elevations received 6.8 inches of new snow over the last 24 hours with wind transport (0.49) active across various aspects. Middle elevations show extreme solar radiation (2239 W/m2) on Southern aspects, resulting in liquid water accumulation (0.44) within the surface layers. Lower elevations show stable conditions with 3.7 inches of new snow, though Southern aspects are beginning to show signs of warming.
forecasts.travel_advice: Limit exposure to steep slopes on Southern aspects as solar loading increases liquid water content. Be wary of new snow loading on steep terrain at upper elevations. The best snow is found on high-elevation Northern aspects that have remained protected from wind and direct solar radiation.

forecasts.primary_concern: Loose wet avalanches on sun-exposed slopes.
forecasts.discussion: Middle elevations are experiencing intense solar radiation (2153 W/m2) on Southern aspects, which is likely to trigger surface warming despite cool ambient temperatures. Lower elevations show significant liquid water content (1.02) on South aspects, indicating a damp and potentially unstable surface snowpack. Northern and Western aspects across all documented bands remain drier and more consolidated.
forecasts.travel_advice: Avoid steep Southern aspects, especially in the afternoon as solar loading peaks and liquid water increases. Focus travel on Northern aspects where the snowpack remains colder and more stable. The highest quality snow is currently situated on mid-elevation Northern slopes.
